step_by_step_thinking:

While broad contact areas can indeed cause issues like food impaction, especially if they're not properly aligned across adjacent surfaces, we should also consider the specific nature of "disto-occlusal" placement mentioned here. In this context, ensuring sufficient clearance from neighboring teeth becomes even more critical because any interference could trap food easily due to the directionality implied by 'disto'. Thus, while option B discusses a similar concept regarding overextension vertically which isn't as relevant given our understanding now about the location being disto-, the primary concern remains broader contacts potentially leading to trapping of food particles.

My final stance aligns closely with choosing Option A - Broad contact area Buccingly.
